title: Bolbophyllaria bracteolata
intro: Bolbophyllaria bracteolata, also known as the 'Queen of the Andes', is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Bolbophyllaria bracteolata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Bolbophyllaria bracteolata is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil to treat various health conditions, and is also used in the production of tinctures and extracts.
taxonomy: Bolbophyllaria bracteolata is a species of orchid native to Mexico. The classification of Bolbophyllaria bracteolata includes being part of the family Orchidaceae. Within this family, Bolbophyllaria bracteolata is further classified into the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Cymbidieae. It is also known as Cymbidium bracteolatum, which is part of the genus Cymbidium. However, some sources classify it as Bolbophyllaria, which is a genus of orchids.
